description: On the Contact page, after filling in the required fields (name, email, message) and clicking Send Message, the form does not submit: validation errors appear as if the fields were empty, and no success message is shown.
expectedOutcome: After entering a valid name, email, and message and clicking Send Message, the form should submit successfully, show a success toast, and clear the form fields.
hint: Handlers that use values from context or current state need to see the latest values when they run. Check how the validation function is defined and what it closes over.
solution: The validate function was wrapped in useCallback with an empty dependency array ([]). That callback captures the initial form state and the initial t (from useTranslation context) from the first render. When the user fills the form and submits, handleSubmit calls this same validate instance, which still references the initial empty form, so validation always fails and the form never submits. Fix: remove useCallback from validate so it is recreated each render and always sees current form and t (and remove useCallback from the React import if unused); or keep useCallback but add the correct dependencies: [form, t].

playwrightTests.name: Contact form submission shows success when required fields are filled
playwrightTests.description: Filling name, email, and message then submitting should result in a success toast and form reset. Fails when bug is present because validation always sees stale (empty) form.
steps: Fill in the name field with a non-empty value
Fill in the email field with a valid email (e.g. user@example.com)
Fill in the message field with a non-empty value
Assert that a success toast/message is visible
Assert that the form fields are cleared
assertions: Expect success toast or success message to be visible after submit
Expect form inputs (name, email, message) to be empty after successful submit

playwrightTests.name: Contact form shows validation errors when required fields are empty
playwrightTests.description: Submitting with empty required fields should show validation errors. Should pass regardless of bug.
steps: Leave name, email, and message empty
Assert that validation error messages are displayed for required fields
assertions: Expect validation error for name to be visible
Expect validation error for email to be visible
Expect validation error for message to be visible

playwrightTests.name: Contact form does not submit with only partial required fields
playwrightTests.description: Filling only some required fields and submitting should show errors and not show success. With bug, even full form fails; this test ensures empty/partial still shows errors.
steps: Assert that validation errors appear and no success toast is shown
assertions: Expect at least one validation error to be visible
Expect success toast not to be visible
